edge_hypothesis: MACD 7:1 tail harvester is the system's best-ever result (PF=1.712, DD=7.5%, 161 trades). MACD time-capped 12-bar at 4:1 R:R produced PF=1.209 with DD=9.5% and 174 trades. The advisory explicitly identifies the intersection — extreme R:R WITH time cap — as untested (Promising Direction #5). The hypothesis: at 7:1 R:R, most losing trades are slow bleeds that wander before eventually hitting the full 2.0x ATR stop. These 'meanderer' trades consume time without producing edge. A 6-8 bar time cap on 1h kills these meanderers by forcing a market exit after N bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit. The explosive winners (genuine trend accelerations) reach 7x TP within 3-5 bars because the MACD histogram zero-cross captures the MOMENTUM of the move — if the move doesn't develop within 6-8 bars, it was a false signal. This should produce: (a) fewer max-loss outcomes (most losers exit at partial loss before hitting full stop), (b) same number of big winners (real moves hit TP fast), (c) improved PF because the loss side improves without degrading the win side. On 4h with 10:1 R:R and 10-bar cap, we test whether slower timeframes need more bars to develop but can reach even more extreme targets. Invalidation: if PF drops below 1.4 vs 1.712 without time cap, the time cap is cutting winners — meaning MACD trends need more than 6-8 bars to develop on 1h. If PF increases above 1.8, time discipline is a universal improvement and should be applied to ALL templates.

variants.name: macd_1h_timecap6_tail_7to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross entry on ETH/1h with 7:1 R:R and aggressive 6-bar time cap. If neither TP (14.0x ATR) nor SL (2.0x ATR) hit within 6 bars, exit at market. The 6-bar cap is aggressive — on 1h, this means the trade has 6 hours to develop. MACD momentum signals that don't produce directional movement within 6 hours are noise. Expected: trade count similar to base MACD 7:1 (~161) but with improved loss profile. Win rate may drop slightly but average loss should decrease significantly.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive — bullish momentum shift).
Close > EMA_50 (price above medium-term trend — bullish alignment).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative — bearish momentum shift).
Close < EMA_50 (price below medium-term trend — bearish alignment).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 6 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.4, the 6-bar cap is killing winners — try 8-bar cap variant
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 6 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend filter — close above/below for directional alignment
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — momentum shift entry signal
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R with 6-bar time cap

variants.name: macd_1h_timecap8_tail_7to1
variants.description: Same MACD 7:1 R:R on ETH/1h but with 8-bar time cap (8 hours). This is the moderate time discipline variant — gives trades 33% more room than the 6-bar version. If 6-bar PF > 8-bar PF, aggressive time caps work better (noise trades die faster). If 8-bar PF > 6-bar PF, MACD trends need 7-8 hours to develop on 1h. The comparison between these two variants reveals the optimal time horizon for MACD momentum signals.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 8 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit).
Hypothesis failure mode: compare PF to 6-bar variant — if 8-bar > 6-bar, extend time cap search to 10-12 bars
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 8 bars at market price.

components.notes: ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R with 8-bar time cap

variants.name: macd_4h_timecap10_extreme_10to1
variants.description: MACD on ETH/4h with extreme 10:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 20.0x TP) and 10-bar time cap (40 hours). This tests the time-cap concept at higher R:R on the slower timeframe. At 10:1 R:R the frontier is unexplored — existing MACD specs tested up to 7:1 (PF=1.712). If the R:R-PF curve is still climbing at 7:1, 10:1 may produce even higher PF. The 10-bar cap on 4h gives 40 hours for the trade to develop — appropriate for the slower timeframe where trends take longer to unfold. Expected: fewer trades than 1h variants (maybe 80-100), but each winner = 10x risk.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 10 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.3 at 10:1, the R:R cliff is between 7:1 and 10:1 — test 8:1 and 9:1 with same time cap
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 10 bars at market price.
